req.title: Message to provider (negative)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-075 requires testing the patient-to-provider messaging flow on /messages with PHI exfiltration scanning for attachments. The suite covers: (1) a positive EP case confirming a valid plain-text message is stored and the provider is notified; (2) a negative NEG case confirming a message with a disallowed PHI attachment pattern is blocked; (3) a boundary BVA case exercising the maximum-length message body exactly at the defined character limit; (4) a state-transition STC case verifying that a message cannot be sent from an unauthenticated/logged-out state; (5) a decision-table DT case combining missing mandatory fields to ensure all disallowed combinations are rejected. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses placeholders for helpers that would live in conftest.

tests.name: test_send_valid_plain_text_message_to_provider
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists and is assigned to patient
actions: POST /messages with valid subject, body, and recipient provider ID
Assert HTTP 201 response
Assert message ID returned in response body
Assert provider notification event emitted
tests.executable:
def test_send_valid_plain_text_message_to_provider(auth_patient_session, provider_fixture, api_client):
    payload = {
        "recipient_id": provider_fixture["id"],
        "subject": "Appointment follow-up",
        "body": "Hello, I have a question about my prescription."
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/messages", json=payload, headers=auth_patient_session)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "message_id" in data
    assert data.get("status") == "delivered"
    note = <get_notification>(data["message_id"])
    assert note["recipient_id"] == provider_fixture["id"]

tests.name: test_send_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Attachment file contains disallowed PHI exfiltration pattern (SSN regex match)
PHI scan service is running and reachable
actions: POST /messages with valid subject and body but attach file containing '123-45-6789' SSN pattern
Assert HTTP 422 or 400 response
Assert error code indicates PHI scan failure
Assert no message record persisted in store
expected: Message is NOT stored in message store
trace: R-HLT-075: blocked when disallowed attachment detected

tests.name: test_send_message_body_at_max_character_boundary
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists and is assigned to patient
System max message body length is defined as MAX_BODY_LEN (e.g. 5000 chars)
actions: POST /messages with body of exactly MAX_BODY_LEN characters
Assert HTTP 201
POST /messages with body of MAX_BODY_LEN + 1 characters
Assert HTTP 400 or 413 with error indicating body too long
expected: Exactly MAX_BODY_LEN body: HTTP 201 and message stored
MAX_BODY_LEN + 1 body: HTTP 400 or 413 with 'BODY_TOO_LONG' error code
trace: R-HLT-075: missing mandatory elements / invalid input (over-boundary)

tests.name: test_send_message_from_unauthenticated_state_is_rejected
preconditions: Patient session has been explicitly logged out (unauthenticated state)
No valid session token present in headers
actions: Attempt POST /messages with no Authorization header
Assert HTTP 401 response
Assert error body indicates authentication required
Confirm no message record is written
expected: Message is NOT stored in message store

tests.name: test_send_message_with_missing_mandatory_fields_combinations
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists and is assigned to patient
actions: POST /messages omitting 'recipient_id' — assert 400
POST /messages omitting 'subject' — assert 400
POST /messages omitting 'body' — assert 400
POST /messages omitting both 'subject' and 'body' — assert 400
expected: Each call returns HTTP 400 Bad Request
No message stored for any of the four calls
